title: Today's AI Brief: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026
summary: A recap of the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026 and open-source software helping robots think

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. This week, I'm catching up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026. The event brought together some of the brightest minds in tech to discuss the latest breakthroughs in AI and how they're shaping our future.

First up, Google's Dialogues stage was a highlight of I/O 2026. The stage featured discussions with Google leaders, scientists, and creative visionaries on the tech that's transforming society. I am looking at the recap, and it seems like a fascinating conversation. They touched on topics beyond the usual AI suspects, which I'm sure sparked some interesting debates.

Next, open-source software is making waves in robotics. A group of academics created open-source robotics hardware, which gave roboticists a lot of time back in their lives. Now, the challenge is getting robots to think, and open-source platforms are stepping up to help. My read is that this could be a game-changer for the field.

Also making headlines, the bigger picture here is that AI is slowly but surely seeping into every aspect of our lives. From robots to dialogues, it's clear that AI is here to stay. What stands out to me is how these advancements are happening in the open, with collaboration and innovation driving progress.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026.
